ROBERT de …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Overview
memoryArtifacts.description:
Robert I (25 September 1216 – 8 February 1250), called the Good, was the first Count of Artois. He was the fifth (and second surviving) son of King Louis VIII of France and Blanche of Castile.[1]

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois於1216年9月25日出生在法國哈帕格-亞登奧布易斯萊-素-拉莫盧普特時，他的父親Louis VIII Roi de France29歲，母親Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia28歲。他於1237年6月14日與Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant在法國皮卡迪奧易可皮艾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1237年在法國阿托斯被列為comte d'artois。他去世於1250年2月8日在埃及الفيوم艾爾 曼斯u拉享年33歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois于1216年9月25日出生在法国哈帕格-亞登奧布易斯萊-素-拉莫盧普特时，他的父亲Louis VIII Roi de France29岁，母亲Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia28岁。他于1237年6月14日与Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant在法国皮卡迪奧易可皮艾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1237年在法国阿托斯被列为comte d'artois。他去世于1250年2月8日在埃及الفيوم艾爾 曼斯u拉享年33岁。

nameMeanings.name: comte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jean Jacques.

nickname for someone in the service of a count or for someone who behaved pretentiously, from Old French conte, cunte ‘count’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lecomte . Compare Conte 3.

variants.name: Lecomte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Monique, Normand, Alain, Armand, Cecile, Dominique, Francois, Germain, Gilles, Gregoire, Huguette, Pierre.

French and Walloon: nickname for someone in the service of a count or for someone who behaved pretentiously, from Old French conte, cunte ‘count’, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Comte 1.
History: This surname is listed (in the form Le Comte) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: 'le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Bon'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered short form of Robert , a later development than the common medieval forms Hob, Dob, and Nob, all of which, unlike Bob, have given rise to English surnames.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois was born on 25 September 1216, in Isle-sous-Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, his father, Louis VIII Roi de France, was 29 and his mother, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, was 28. He married Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant on 14 June 1237, in Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. In 1237, his occupation is listed as comte d'artois in Artois, France. He died on 8 February 1250, in Al Manşūrah, Faiyum, Egypt, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois est né le 25 septembre 1216, à Isle-sous-Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, son père, Louis VIII Roi de France, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant le 14 juin 1237, à Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. En 1237, sa profession de son indiquée est comte d'artois à Artois, France. Il est décédé le 8 février 1250, à Al Manşūrah, Faiyum, Égypte, à l’âge de 33 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois am 25. September 1216 in Isle-sous-Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Louis VIII Roi de France 29 und seine Mutter Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia 28. Er hat am 14. Juni 1237 in Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, Frankreich Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1237 ist sein Beruf als comte d'artois in Artois, Frankreich aufgeführt. Er starb am 8. Februar 1250 im Alter von 33 Jahren in Al Manşūrah, Faiyum, Ägypten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois è nato il il 25 settembre 1216 nel Isle-sous-Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, il suo padre, Louis VIII Roi de France, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant il 14 giugno 1237, nel Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1237, la sua occupazione è indicata come comte d'artois nel Artois, Francia. Morì il 8 febbraio 1250, nel Al Manşūrah, al-Fayyum, Egitto, all'età di 33 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artoisが1216年9月25日にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアウベイスレ-ソウス-ラムルプトで生まれた時，父Louis VIII Roi de Franceは29歳，母Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Franciaは28歳であった。1237年6月14日にフランスピカーディオイゼコンパeグンでMatilda, countess of artois of Brabantと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。1237年，フランスアルトイスでの職業はcomte d'artoisと記録されている。彼は1250年2月8日にエジプトالفيومアル マンsuラで33歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois(이)가 1216년 9월 25일 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 오브 이슬레-소우스-레이머업트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Louis VIII Roi de France(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1237년 6월 14일 프랑스 피카디 이스 콤피e그니에서 Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1237년, 그의 직업은 프랑스 아토이스에서 comte d'artois이었다. 그는 1250년 2월 8일 이집트 الفيوم 앨 먼su라에서 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.25 September 1216–8 February 1250: 25 de setembro de 1216–8 de fevereiro de 1250
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois nasceu em 25 de setembro de 1216, em Isle-sous-Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, França, seu pai, Louis VIII Roi de France, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant em 14 de junho de 1237, em Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1237, sua profissão aparece como comte d'artois em Artois, França. Ele faleceu em 8 de fevereiro de 1250, em Al Manşūrah, Faiyum, Egipto, com 33 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois родился 25 сентября 1216 г. в местности исле-соус-рамирупт, ауб, чампагне-арденн, Франция, его отцу по имени Louis VIII Roi de France было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant 14 июня 1237 г. в местности компиeгн, оис, пикарди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. В 1237 году он работал по профессии comte d'artois в местности артоис, Франция. Он умер 8 февраля 1250 г. в местности аль манsuрах, الفيوم, Египет в возрасте 33 лет.

sm.from 1237 to 1250: mai le 1237 e o'o I le 1250

es.25 September 1216–8 February 1250: 25 de septiembre de 1216–8 de febrero de 1250
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Ier 'le Bon' comte d'Artois nació el 25 de septiembre de 1216, en Isle-sous-Ramerupt, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, su papá, Louis VIII Roi de France, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Princesa Blanca de Castilla Reina Consorte de Francia, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Matilda, countess of artois of Brabant el 14 de junio de 1237, en Compiègne, Oise, Picardie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1237, su ocupación registrada era comte d'artois en Artois, Francia. Murió el 8 de febrero de 1250, en Al Manşūrah, Faiyum, Egipto, a la edad de 33 años.
